data.title: CFTC's K. Johnson exits, warns about oversight gaps, as agency approves previously banned platforms.
data.description: As the regulatory official prepares to leave their position, they have issued a final caution regarding gaps in oversight. This warning comes at a time when the agency has approved the return of platforms that were previously banned. The regulator highlighted the need for improved monitoring and control measures. This announcement follows the approval of [specific number] platforms that had been prohibited from operating. The departing official emphasized the importance of addressing regulatory shortcomings before allowing these platforms to resume their services.

data.title: Regenx updates on Management Cease Trade Order bi-weekly; annual filings by Sept 29, 2025, audit ongoing with DMCL, no material changes.
data.description: Regenx has recently provided an update every two weeks on the Management Cease Trade Order (MCTO), with an ongoing audit being conducted by DMCL. The company anticipates filing its annual reports by September 29, 2025. There have been no significant developments or alterations disclosed so far.

data.title: Aurzen debuts ZIP Cyber Edition projector at IFA 2025, global unveiling of flagship projector, first worldwide showcase.
data.description: Aurzen's latest innovation, the ZIP Cyber Edition projector, was revealed at IFA 2025 in Berlin. This flagship product signifies a pivotal moment for the company, as it is a global first release. The projector boasts cutting-edge features that promise an unparalleled viewing experience. The launch has generated significant buzz in the tech industry and among consumers.

data.title: Mobix Labs wins contract to develop AI drone tech for U.S. rail industry, entering multi-billion-dollar market.
data.description: On September 4, 2025, Mobix Labs, Inc. revealed that its Mobix Labs Wireless division, RaGE Systems, secured a new contract to design drone-based, AI-enhanced inspection technology for the U.S. rail sector. This move signifies the company's expansion into a significant market, utilizing its expertise to enhance rail safety and enhance vital infrastructure. Mobix Labs, listed on Nasdaq as MOBX, specializes in cutting-edge connectivity and sensing solutions, and the recent contract with RaGE Systems underscores its commitment to innovation in the field. The new initiative aims to revolutionize inspection processes within the U.S. rail industry, demonstrating Mobix Labs' dedication to leveraging advanced technology for societal benefit and industrial progress.

data.title: Palantir and Lear expand partnership for 5 years to enhance automotive tech transformation globally. #PLTR #LEA #AI
data.description: In Denver, Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR), a prominent AI software provider, has revealed a five-year extension of its collaboration with Lear Corporation (NYSE: LEA), a key player in the automotive industry specializing in Seating and E-Systems. This renewed agreement will see Lear expanding its utilization of Palantir Foundry, alongside Palantir's Warp Speed manufacturing operating system and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP), throughout its worldwide manufacturing operations. Foundry and AIP will be integrated across Lear's global manufacturing sites as per this enhanced partnership.

data.title: Poland construction industry to grow by 2.4% in 2025, with output projected to increase at AAGR of 4% from 2026-2029. Government infrastructure investments and renewable energy targets aim for 56% by 2030.
data.description: A recent report on the construction market in Poland indicates positive growth prospects. The Polish construction industry is projected to expand by 2.4% in 2025, with notable investments focused on areas impacted by floods. Additionally, there is a surge in tourism activities contributing to this growth. The report covers various sectors including commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy, utilities, institutional, and residential markets. This analysis extends up to 2029, providing insights into market trends and forecasts. The data has been sourced from ResearchAndMarkets.com.

data.title: ETHFI price remains at $1.12 after $100M institutional investment, up 2.56%, as ETHZilla's announcement boosts bullish sentiment. Technical outlook uncertain.
data.description: The cryptocurrency ETHFI is currently trading at $1.12, marking a 2.56% increase in daily gains after ETHZilla unveiled a significant $100 million deployment plan. As per technical analysis, indicators are showing a combination of positive and negative signals for the future trajectory of ETHFI.

data.title: Coinbase CEO wants over 50% of daily code AI-generated. Currently, AI generates 40%, aiming for 50% by October.
data.description: Coinbase Global Inc.'s CEO, Brian Armstrong, is aiming to increase the percentage of AI-generated code at the company to over 50% within the next month. Currently, AI already generates about 40% of the daily code at Coinbase, and Armstrong wants to raise this number to surpass 50% by October. He emphasized the importance of responsible use of AI-generated code, acknowledging that not all areas of the business may be suitable for it. Armstrong believes that maximizing the use of AI-generated code is crucial for the company. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: IFF's DEB platform used in large-scale for home care, enhancing laundry detergent with improved fabric softness and cleaning. NYSE: IFF.
data.description: A leading company in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients, health, and biosciences, IFF (NYSE: IFF), has introduced its Designed Enzymatic Biomaterials™ (DEB) platform at a significant scale with a major consumer packaged goods (CPG) corporation utilizing a fresh laundry detergent formula. This updated formula, integrating DEB technology, offers enhanced fabric softness and cleaning efficiency. Noteworthy is the replacement of non-biodegradable components with easily biodegradable alternatives, marking a sustainable shift in the industry.

data.title: Coinbase CEO aims for 50% of code from AI by October amid rising interest in AI-assisted coding.
data.description: The CEO of a cryptocurrency exchange has announced a goal for 50% of their daily code to be generated by artificial intelligence, reflecting the increasing popularity of AI-assisted programming. This shift highlights a significant trend in the industry as companies look to utilize AI technologies for coding purposes.

data.title: SAIC reports second quarter FY2026 results, with revenue totaling $2.5 billion, showing growth in defense and intelligence sectors.
data.description: In Reston, Virginia on September 4, 2025, Science Applications International Corporation (NASDAQ: SAIC) disclosed its second-quarter outcomes for the period ending on August 1, 2025. SAIC, a top Fortune 500 technology company, specializes in advancing the nation's digital evolution within defense, space, civilian, and intelligence sectors.

data.title: EigenLayer (EIGEN) hovers around $1.21 as technical signs show mixed momentum. RSI at 46.26 suggests weak bullish trend.
data.description: The price of EIGEN is currently trading at $1.21 on the Binance spot market. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) stands at 46.26, indicating a neutral position. Despite this, there is a weak bullish trend observed as the price remains below important moving averages.

data.title: Japan’s regulator suggests updating rules for cryptocurrencies to align with securities law, transferring oversight from the Payment Services Act to the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.
data.description: The Financial Services Agency in Japan has suggested transferring the regulation of cryptocurrency from the Payment Services Act to the more rigorous Financial Instruments and Exchange Act. This move aims to enhance oversight and control over crypto-related activities in the country. The proposed shift reflects a strategic effort to bolster regulations in the cryptocurrency sector by subjecting it to stricter rules. This change would significantly impact the oversight of cryptocurrencies in Japan, potentially leading to more stringent monitoring and enforcement.

data.title: Thomson Reuters sets up $1.0 billion share repurchase plan and NCIB to buy up to 10 million shares.
data.description: Today, Thomson Reuters (TSX/Nasdaq: TRI) revealed their plan for a $1.0 billion share repurchase program and normal course issuer bid (NCIB) to acquire up to 10 million shares. This initiative comes as part of the company's efforts to enhance shareholder value and optimize their capital structure. Thomson Reuters, a prominent global content and technology firm, is taking strategic steps to solidify its position in the market.

data.title: Waterdrop Inc., a tech platform focusing on insurance and healthcare, reported net profit of RMB140.2 million in Q2 2025, a 58.7% YoY increase. Insurance premiums rose sharply at RMB3,204.3 million, operating revenue increased to RMB838.0 million. They launched AI applications improving user experience and revenue, reported 999 patient enrollments, and issued US$10.9 million cash dividend. Cash position as of June 30, 2025, was RMB3,406.7 million. Additionally, they have repurchased 55.7 million ADSs for US$109.0 million, and initiated a new share repurchase program. Shareholders to receive a cash dividend of approximately US$10.9 million.
data.description: Waterdrop Inc. reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2025, with a net profit of RMB 140.2 million (US$19.6 million), up 58.7% YoY. Insurance premiums reached RMB 3,204.3 million (US$447.3 million), a 80.2% increase YoY. Medical crowdfunding saw 480 million people donate RMB 70.0 billion to 3.54 million patients. The company enrolled 12,216 patients into clinical trials. Operating revenue rose by 23.9% to RMB 838.0 million (US$117.0 million). Operating costs increased by 18.7%, sales and marketing expenses rose by 26.3%, and general and administrative expenses decreased by 21.9%. Operating profit was RMB 97.3 million (US$13.6 million). The cash position stood at RMB 3,406.7 million (US$475.6 million). The company has repurchased 55.7 million ADSs for US$109.0 million and approved a new share repurchase program up to US$50 million. A cash dividend of US$0.03 per ADS or US$0.003 per ordinary share, totaling around US$10.9 million, was approved. The company aims for top and bottom-line growth in the second half of 2025 with a focus on tech-driven innovation.

data.title: Recall.ai secures $38M in Series B funding, reaching $250M valuation; expanding conversation data solutions.
data.description: Recall.ai, a San Francisco-based company known for its API for meeting recordings, has recently secured $38 million in Series B funding. This funding round has valued the company at $250 million. Bessemer Venture Partners led the funding, with investments also coming in from HubSpot Ventures and Salesforce Ventures. The company plans to use this capital injection to diversify its offerings, moving beyond meeting integrations and introducing new features such as the Desktop Recording SDK, dialers, phone, and in-person conversation data capture capabilities.

data.title: Agentic automation highlighted at ABBYY Ascend Fall event in Austin on October 14, 2025.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, ABBYY Ascend Fall on October 14, 2025, is set to showcase the latest advancements in agentic automation. This event will highlight the future of automation, with a focus on empowering businesses through innovative technology.
